task_completion.description: The follow-up MRI volume is loaded into the Slicer workspace.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Data module, Subject Hierarchy, or slice view annotations show 'followup_flair_misaligned' is loaded.

task_completion.description: The registration module is configured to align the follow-up to the baseline.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'General Registration (BRAINS)' module is visible. Fixed image is set to the baseline FLAIR. Moving image is set to the follow-up. Registration type includes 'Rigid' or 6 DOF.

task_completion.description: The agent visually verifies the alignment of the two volumes.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Slice views show the two volumes superimposed (e.g., using a checkerboard filter, transparency overlay, or toggling visibility) where anatomical structures appear aligned.

task_completion.description: The lesion diameter is measured using the Markups/Ruler tool.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A ruler line (MarkupsLine) is drawn across the hyperintense (bright) lesion area in a slice view.

task_completion.description: The registered volume and transform files are saved.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Save Data' dialog or a file explorer shows 'followup_registered.nii.gz' and 'followup_transform.h5' being saved or present.

task_completion.description: A JSON report containing the measurements is created.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal shows the content of 'followup_report.json' including 'baseline_diameter_mm' and 'followup_diameter_mm' fields.

integrity.description: The agent uses the 3D Slicer GUI modules rather than only scripting.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with Slicer panels, buttons, and menus (e.g., Registration module, Markups toolbar).

integrity.description: The report values are derived from actual tool usage, not hallucinated.
integrity.visual_evidence: The measurement values in the report correspond roughly to the ruler length shown in the slice views (if visible).

integrity.description: The agent is interacting with the actual 3D Slicer application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The interface matches the standard 3D Slicer layout (Four-up view, module panel on the left).

privileged_info_for_vlm: The input file is 'followup_flair_misaligned.nii.gz'. The expected output files are 'followup_registered.nii.gz', 'followup_transform.h5', and 'followup_report.json'. The lesion appears as a bright (hyperintense) spot on the MRI. Successful registration should result in the follow-up scan matching the orientation and position of the baseline scan visually. The registration type must be Rigid (6 DOF).
